# US-004: Reorder tasks by priority - Implementation Plan

**Generated by:** Archetipo Planning Team
**Date:** 2026-05-06

---

## User Story

**Epic:** EP-001 - Sprint & Backlog Management
**Priority:** HIGH | **Story Points:** 3

**Story**
As Valentina, I want to reorder backlog tasks via drag-and-drop or by setting an explicit position, so that the most important tasks appear first when each developer opens their daily view.

**Acceptance Criteria**
- [ ] Dragging a task in the backlog list and dropping it at a new position persists the new order
- [ ] User can also set a numeric priority position via an inline input
- [ ] Order is preserved across page reloads and sessions
- [ ] Reordering is restricted to tasks within the same sprint

---

## Technical Solution

We add a single canonical persistence path - a `reorderTasksAction(sprintId, orderedTaskIds[])` server action that rewrites every task's `position` column in one transaction - and feed it from two UI surfaces: a drag-and-drop list powered by `@dnd-kit/sortable` and an inline 1-based numeric input on each row. Both modalities compute the new full ordering client-side and call the same action, so persistence has a single contract regardless of input modality. The `Task.position` field already exists from US-003, so no schema migration is required. Reordering is constrained to one sprint by validating, on the server, that all submitted task IDs belong to the same `sprintId` owned by the caller. Optimistic updates with rollback-on-error keep the UI responsive without blocking on round-trips. This story has a hard dependency on US-003 (`Task` model and `/sprints/[id]` page) being merged first.

- **New dependencies**: add `@dnd-kit/core`, `@dnd-kit/sortable`, `@dnd-kit/utilities`. Chosen over `react-beautiful-dnd` (unmaintained) and HTML5 native DnD (poor a11y, no touch) because dnd-kit ships built-in keyboard sensor and screen-reader announcements, satisfying basic accessibility for free.
- **Validation**: `src/lib/validation/task-order.ts` exports `reorderTasksSchema` requiring `sprintId` (uuid), `orderedTaskIds` (non-empty array of unique uuids, max 200). Shared by server action and client.
- **Server action `reorderTasksAction`** in `src/app/sprints/[id]/actions.ts`: authenticates via `getCurrentUser()`, asserts `sprint.ownerId === user.id`, fetches all tasks for that sprint, rejects unless the submitted ID set matches the persisted set exactly (no missing, extra, or cross-sprint IDs), then in a single `prisma.$transaction` updates each task's `position` to its index in the array. `revalidatePath('/sprints/[id]')` on success.
- **Sprint-detail page wiring**: `/sprints/[id]/page.tsx` continues to fetch tasks ordered by `position asc, createdAt asc` (already established in US-003) and passes them to a new `SortableTaskList` client component instead of the static `TaskList`.
- **`SortableTaskList` (client)**: wraps the rows in `<DndContext>` + `<SortableContext>`, holds the local ordered task state, calls `reorderTasksAction` after a drop or after a numeric-input commit. Optimistic: applies the new order immediately, rolls back and shows a `sonner` error toast on failure. Pointer + keyboard sensors enabled.
- **`SortableTaskRow` (client)**: extends the existing US-003 task row with (1) a left-side drag handle (grip icon, `cursor: grab`) that owns the dnd-kit listeners, leaving the rest of the row free for clicks on edit/delete; (2) a small numeric `Input` showing the 1-based position. The input commits on `Enter` or blur: it clamps to `[1, n]`, computes the new ordering by removing the row from its current index and re-inserting at `newIndex - 1`, then triggers the same persistence path as DnD.
- **No-op short-circuit**: if the new ordering equals the current one (drop in place, or numeric input unchanged), the action is not called.

---

## Test Strategy

We unit-test the pure ordering helper and the Zod schema, integration-test the server action's authorization and atomicity, and e2e-test both input modalities including keyboard-driven drag for accessibility. The "scoped to one sprint" guarantee is verified explicitly with cross-sprint IDs.

- **Unit** - `reorderTasksSchema` rejects empty array, duplicates, non-uuid entries, oversize arrays.
- **Unit** - pure helper `moveTaskToPosition(orderedIds, taskId, newIndex)` returns the correctly reordered array, clamps out-of-range positions, and is idempotent when newIndex equals current index.
- **Integration** - `reorderTasksAction` rewrites positions atomically (verified by re-querying tasks and checking the new sequence); rejects unauthenticated calls; rejects when the sprint is owned by another user; rejects when the submitted ID set is missing one of the sprint's tasks, contains an extra ID, or contains an ID belonging to a different sprint; on database error inside the transaction, no positions are changed.
- **Integration** - reordering is a no-op (no DB writes) when the submitted order equals the persisted order.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - authenticated user drags a task from position 3 to position 1, sees the row move, reloads the page, and the new order persists.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - user types `1` into the position input of the last task, presses Enter, sees the row jump to the top, reloads, order persists.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - keyboard drag: user tabs to the drag handle, presses Space, uses Arrow keys to move, presses Space again to drop; the order persists.
- **E2E (Playwright)** - typing an out-of-range position (e.g. `99` in a 5-task list) is clamped to the last position.

| Status | # | Task | Description | Type | Dependencies |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| DONE | TASK-01 | Add @dnd-kit dependencies | Install `@dnd-kit/core`, `@dnd-kit/sortable`, `@dnd-kit/utilities`; commit lockfile. | Impl | - |
| DONE | TASK-02 | Create reorder validation schema | Add `src/lib/validation/task-order.ts` exporting `reorderTasksSchema` (sprintId uuid, non-empty unique uuid array, max 200). | Impl | - |
| DONE | TASK-03 | Unit tests for schema and ordering helper | Cover empty/duplicate/oversize arrays for the schema; cover move-to-position helper edge cases (clamp, no-op, move up/down). | Test | TASK-02 |
| DONE | TASK-04 | Implement `reorderTasksAction` | Add server action in `src/app/sprints/[id]/actions.ts`: auth, ownership check, full-set ID validation, `$transaction` rewrite of `position`, `revalidatePath`. | Impl | TASK-02 |
| DONE | TASK-05 | Integration tests for server action | Atomic rewrite happy path, unauth/cross-user/cross-sprint/missing/extra ID rejections, transaction rollback, no-op short-circuit. | Test | TASK-04 |
| DONE | TASK-06 | Build `SortableTaskList` client component | Wrap rows in `DndContext` + `SortableContext` with pointer+keyboard sensors, hold optimistic local state, persist via `reorderTasksAction`, rollback + toast on error. | Impl | TASK-01, TASK-04 |
| DONE | TASK-07 | Build `SortableTaskRow` with drag handle and position input | Extend the US-003 row with left-side grip handle (dnd-kit listeners) and inline 1-based numeric input that commits on Enter/blur via the same persistence path. | Impl | TASK-06 |
| DONE | TASK-08 | Wire `SortableTaskList` into sprint-detail page | Replace `TaskList` with `SortableTaskList` in `/sprints/[id]/page.tsx`; tasks continue to be fetched ordered by position. | Impl | TASK-07 |
| DONE | TASK-09 | E2E tests for reorder flow | Playwright: pointer drag, numeric input commit, keyboard drag (a11y), out-of-range clamp, persistence after reload. | Test | TASK-08 |
